purpose: Lightweight routing + output-contract fixtures. These are specs, not executed against a live model in CI. tests/test_routing_fixtures.py validates that each fixture is well-formed and routes to a real skill; they also serve as worked examples of when deep-research is (and is NOT) the right skill, and what its output must contain.

fixtures.request: Write a thorough, cited report on the state of solid-state battery commercialization in 2026 — the key players, expected timelines, and the remaining technical barriers.

expected_routing.reason: Open-ended multi-source synthesis into a single cited report. Not a recurring digest, not a dossier on one named entity, not Q&A over user-supplied sources.

fixtures.request: Is it true that more than half of new cars sold in Norway in 2025 were fully electric? Check this and show your sources.

expected_routing.reason: A single, specific claim to verify with a confidence rating goes to fact-checker; deep-research handles broad multi-question topics (its fact-check report category covers claims that surface DURING a wider report).

fixtures.request: Give me a background dossier on Acme Robotics Ltd — who owns it, any adverse media, and whether it has sanctions exposure.

expected_routing.reason: Background/vetting of one named entity with ownership, adverse-media and sanctions signals is entity-research, not a general research report.

fixtures.request: I'm going to paste five PDFs. Answer my questions strictly from those documents, with quotes, and tell me when they don't cover something.

expected_routing.reason: Strictly-grounded Q&A over user-supplied sources is notebooklm-mode (vault), not open-web deep research.
